Hey, what's up everyone? This is the text reminder sequence, so this is what happens after the person has confirmed their time or has scheduled with you. So you should have already made contact with the person and you're either confirming or got their appointment time scheduled. This is what happens between then and the show up. Alright, so a quick reminder, everything should always be same day next day. Same day next day is like everything, if you can't get people scheduled same day next day, the likelihood they show up is significantly lower. That's the first thing I always ask if someone had low show rates. As a quick primer on that, if you have people that are going too far out, you just have to ask them, hey, when were you planning on working out? And then usually they'll say, well, after work, it's like, cool, well, cool, let's just do that tomorrow. And so that's the point is that if they can make time to work out, which hopefully they should be working out three days a week, if they're working out three days a week every other day, then you should be able to find a time same day next day to get them in. And so if someone's like, well, I can only work out, you know, I can only come in next Wednesday, you know what I mean? And it's Wednesday of the week before it's like, well, when were you planning on working out? And then, you know, they'll tell you like, well, I was planning on working out after work, cool, can we just do that tomorrow? And then just go from there, believe it or not, that actually works all the time. Okay, so the two days that matter most because most of your schedule should be same day next day, then the two reminder time periods are the day before and the day of. All right. So the day before this is super elite above and beyond a personal video text saying like, hey, Sally, how's it going? It's Alex. I just wanted to drop you a line, let you know that you're going to be on my team. So you're coming tomorrow as my personal guest. All right. So I hope you haven't forgotten about us because I have not forgotten about you. So looking forward to meeting you tomorrow. It's going to be awesome. And I will see you then. And then that's it. All right. Easy peasy. It's just a reminder text. And I probably should have said the time and the appointment. So you want to put the time in there too. And then that is a super duper. If you want to be lazy, then just text them, hey, friendly reminder about your and the name of the program 42 did detox appointment tomorrow at 4pm with Alex. That's me. Smiley face at address. See you then. That's it. All right. That's the day before. I prefer to do these at night. Okay. So I prefer to send these messages between eight and nine o'clock. That was the best time because people are scheduling their day out for the next day when they get that if you text these during the day, they will forget. If you text them at night, they're thinking about the next day and they will wake up with that intention. Okay. Now, same day. So this is now the day of the appointment. All right. So this is superior call first thing in the morning. If they pick up, then confirm over the phone that you're going to be with them, the appointment, be friendly, etc. Okay. If they do not pick up on that call, then send a personal video text. So this is in the morning, eight o'clock, nine o'clock, etc. All right. So hey name, right? Hey, Susie. You're like, hey Susie, just want to let you know we're stuck to see you tonight. A reminder that you don't need to bring workout stuff. This is a no sweat. Just get to know your session so you can relax. I'm not going to make you do anything besides just figure out your goals and see how we can out. Cool. Okay. Oh yeah. Text me back your preferred shirt size so I can pull one of those aside for you. All right. I'll see you tonight. Boom. Boom. And that's it. Now, the hour before ish, right? I mean, like if you know you have a whole bunch of sales appointments and you're not going to text, you know, if you have, let's say your sales time is from between four and eight, right? Then send all these texts out between three and four. All right. And then don't worry about it. But so that's why it says one hour before ish. So basically in the afternoons, you know, before their appointments, this is when you would do it. You're like, Sally, just a heads up. We're the building across the XYZ landmark. If you have any trouble finding us, don't hesitate to give me a holler. I'll come out and get you. All right. And then that would be a picture of you pointing at the front of your facility, usually making a funny face or something like that. All right. And so that way it's kind of, it's fluid from the day before, the morning of, and then kind of an hour or two out, just being like, Hey, that's the fun of the building. If you have trouble finding, just let me know. I'll come out and get you. All right. That is it. That is the process that you need to take people through. And a quick note on automated reminders. We have shown you how to build out automated reminders for their appointments. I still, still strongly, strongly, strongly believe that people do not respond to automated reminders. It's good to have them because they will remember, but they do not come because of the automated reminders. The automated reminders are truly there to remind them, but automated reminders do not make people care. All right. And so the nurture process that we do, given the personalized videos and the funny pictures and things like that, the smiley faces is there to make them care about the thing that you will remind them of. Okay. That's the point. So this is where we're using systematization instead of automation to accomplish the same goal. All right. So that is the process. That is the manual nurture process. As it stands right now, it's the most effective way that we've gotten to get people in the door in large droves. Okay. So block out the time at night, the day before, and block out the time in the morning and then 30 minutes-ish before your afternoon appointments. It does not take long. You just have to rip it off like a mandate and get it done. All right. Awesome.
